text.title: Feedback Management Examples
text.summary: Monthly feedback review cycle and app-store review response strategy as concrete worked examples of the six-stage pipeline in action.

example.note: Total feedback: 150 items across all channels in the month.
Top category by volume: Integrations (40%). Top request: Slack integration (25 mentions across support, social, and in-app).
Emerging trend: mobile app requests increasing from 5% to 15% share over 3 months.

Actions taken: Slack integration added to Q2 roadmap (25 Slack requesters notified by email); mobile scheduled for discovery (3 interviews booked); 3 billing bugs fixed (resolved items closed in register).
Close loop: email sent to the 25 users who requested Slack integration, quoting their original verbatim and linking to the roadmap.
1-2 star reviews: respond within 24 hours, apologize and offer help, move to direct support channel. Log the core complaint in the feedback register.
3-4 star reviews: thank, ask "what would make it 5 stars?", note the answer in the feedback log.
5-star reviews: thank, consider for referral or marketing testimonial (with permission).

antipattern.description: Auto-creating backlog items from every feedback ticket without a clustering step.
antipattern.reason: Individual feedback tickets flood the backlog with 1-mention items; require at least 3 mentions of the same theme before issue creation to avoid noise.

antipattern.description: Building features based on mention count from free-tier users while ignoring silent enterprise accounts.
antipattern.reason: Free-tier vocal users generate disproportionate feedback volume but represent a fraction of revenue; segment weighting by ARR corrects this bias.

antipattern.description: Never responding to users about the fate of their requests.
antipattern.reason: Users who receive no close-loop response assume their feedback is ignored; declining to build a feature with an honest reason maintains more trust than silence.

antipattern.description: Feedback stored in multiple disconnected tools (Slack, email, Notion, support tool) with no central aggregation.
antipattern.reason: Siloed feedback prevents pattern detection; the same critical issue can appear in support, social, and NPS simultaneously without any human seeing the full picture.
